tests.intent: The reviewed bound table-macro interface and its five fixed output columns exist as valid Oracle objects.
Every render contains exactly 64,000 unique coordinate rows and all three semantic layers are represented.
Every column 0 through 319 owns every row 0 through 199 exactly once, without gaps, duplicates, or out-of-range coordinates.
Every canonical output is a non-null integer palette index in the inclusive range zero through 255.
A hand-authored patterned mini-map independently fixes projection boundaries and floor, ceiling, wall palette pixels.
Pinned E1M1 spawn facing east matches independent layer counts, spot pixels, and the complete raw-byte frame hash.
Pinned E1M1 spawn facing north matches independent layer counts, spot pixels, and the complete raw-byte frame hash.
Pinned E1M1 spawn facing south matches independent layer counts, spot pixels, and the complete raw-byte frame hash.
Solid walls sample dense relational texture texels using facing side, seg distance, and deterministic role selection.
Floor pixels use half-row reverse projection, negative-safe floor modulus, and the nearest hit facing sector flat.
Ceiling pixels use half-row reverse projection, negative-safe floor modulus, and the nearest hit facing sector flat.
All semantic layers pass raw texels through the floored and clamped sector-light COLORMAP band.
Wall sampling honors seg and sidedef x offsets, sidedef y offset, and upper/lower unpegged alignment.
R1 deliberately uses the nearest solid facing sector for floor and ceiling and documents that temporary limitation.
Two distinct bound session tokens with identical pose and state produce byte-identical 64,000-pixel frames.
Repeated evaluation in one database session produces identical rows and the same canonical frame hash.
Four complete independent frames are SHA-256 hashed over palette bytes in column-major coordinate order.
Production rendering is bound relational SQL with no procedural pixel or wall loops, recursion, or dynamic SQL.
Production contains no fixture poses, expected hashes, evaluator paths, caller inspection, test flags, or reference output.
All isolated semantic renderer mutations have an explicit independent witness and named detecting test.
